title: Phase0 T3: Implement hooked coverage subcommand
description: Add a Rust subcommand to hooked. Target files: src/cmd/coverage.rs (new), src/cli.rs, src/cmd/mod.rs, src/lib.rs. Context: src/cmd/agents.rs:76-88 (handler pattern), src/cmd/tail.rs:70-127 (live-JSONL read), src/envelope.rs:126-171 (parse_jsonl_file), src/cli.rs:64-135 (Command enum), src/render.rs:160-190 (JSON render).
acceptance_criteria: hooked coverage <agent_id> --json reads recent live JSONL logs, filters events where p.agent_id==<id>, and emits deduped reads (file paths), globs, greps, dirs. A --write flag writes the same JSON to ~/.claude/telemetry/coverage/<agent_id>.json. cargo build and cargo test pass.

title: Phase0 follow-up: verify live hook triggers next session (auto-write + gate)
description: Both the SubagentStop coverage auto-write (T4) and the PreToolUse coverage gate (T5) were wired into ~/.claude/settings.json this session; Claude Code does not hot-reload hooks mid-session, so neither fired live yet. Their CODE is verified (cargo tests + functional tests via build-runner; coverage --write proven end-to-end).
acceptance_criteria: In a NEW session: (1) spawn a search subagent, then confirm ~/.claude/telemetry/coverage/<that-agent-id>.json is auto-created. (2) Have a worker edit a file no explorer read this session; confirm the additionalContext coverage warning appears AND the edit is NOT blocked. (3) Confirm a normal worker edit to a covered file produces no warning and is not blocked.

title: Phase0 T8: Final review of all Phase 0 changes
description: Review all Phase 0 changes for correctness and safety. Target files: hooked src/cmd/coverage.rs, src/cli.rs; ~/.claude/hooks/coverage-autowrite.sh, coverage-gate.sh; ~/.claude/settings.json; ~/.claude/agents/{search,orchestrator,worker}.md; ~/.claude/references/handoff-provenance.md.
acceptance_criteria: Review confirms: Rust subcommand correctness + test coverage; the gate is genuinely non-destructive (cannot hard-block normal edits); SubagentStop auto-write correctly filtered by agent_type; prompt contracts internally consistent and not contradicting bd/proportionality rules; no telemetry PII/secret leakage in coverage files.

title: Phase0 T6: Wire producer + consumer prompt contracts
description: Edit ~/.claude/agents/search.md (producer), ~/.claude/agents/orchestrator.md and ~/.claude/agents/worker.md (consumer). Cite the T2 reference (~/.claude/references/handoff-provenance.md). Context: orchestrator.md:131-156 (Verify Delegated Work).
acceptance_criteria: search.md report format requires per-finding confidence tags + mandatory PREMISE CHECK; orchestrator.md gains provenance-handling: after each explorer returns, Read ~/.claude/telemetry/coverage/<agentId>.json, treat [inferred]/[unverified] as re-verify-before-use, treat out-of-coverage areas as 'unknown not clear', non-empty PREMISE CHECK forces plan re-eval; worker.md consumes provenance and acknowledges out-of-coverage edits (ties to T5 marker). All three cite the same reference with consistent terms.

title: Phase0 T7: Build measurement harness (Phase 0 success gate)
description: Repeatable metric over telemetry reporting (a) re-exploration overlap (fraction of files a worker Reads that an explorer already Read in the same session) and (b) coverage-edge incidents (worker Edits outside explorer observed coverage). Implementer chooses Rust subcommand in hooked or extension to ~/.claude/skills/p:analyse-session/scripts/analyze_telemetry.py. Context: src/schema.rs:40-149, src/envelope.rs:126-171.
acceptance_criteria: Metric computes re-exploration overlap and coverage-edge incidents from telemetry; a baseline can be computed from existing historical logs before rollout; output is machine-readable. Build/smoke check passes.
notes: Re-scoped Python->Rust: implement as a hooked subcommand (provenance) so it is cargo-test + cargo-run verifiable via build-runner without relaxing the hook guard. Delete the superseded ~/.claude/telemetry/provenance_metrics.py.

title: Phase0 T5: Implement acknowledgement-style coverage gate
description: PreToolUse Edit|Write|MultiEdit hook that warns/asks (per T1) when a target file is outside the active task's observed-coverage manifest and no ack marker is present. Target files: ~/.claude/hooks/coverage-gate.sh (new), ~/.claude/settings.json. Context: enforce-orchestrator.sh:40-54 (marker+deny output format), settings.json:203-220 (matcher structure), T1 determination, ~/.claude/telemetry/coverage/ files.
acceptance_criteria: Hook emits the signal chosen in T1 (warn/ask, NOT unconditional deny) when editing outside observed coverage without ack. New-file creation and acknowledged edits pass cleanly. Smoke test confirms it does NOT block normal worker edits.

title: Phase0 T4: Wire SubagentStop auto-write for explorer agents
description: On SubagentStop, invoke hooked coverage <agent_id> --write, filtered to explorer agent_types. Target files: ~/.claude/settings.json (SubagentStop hook array), ~/.claude/hooks/coverage-autowrite.sh (new). Context: settings.json:222-256 (SubagentStop entry), enforce-orchestrator.sh:15-25 (stdin/jq parsing).
acceptance_criteria: On SubagentStop, hook reads payload and ONLY when agent_type in {search,ast-search,lsp-search,worker} invokes hooked coverage <agent_id> --write. Verified by spawning a search subagent and confirming ~/.claude/telemetry/coverage/<agent_id>.json appears with correct contents; non-explorer agents produce no file.
notes: RESIDUAL VERIFICATION (kept in_progress, honest): coverage-autowrite.sh + settings.json SubagentStop edit are read-verified, and its core command (hooked coverage <id> --write) was proven end-to-end by build-runner. The ONLY unverified link is the live SubagentStop hook trigger, which requires Claude Code to reload settings.json (activates next session; not hot-reloadable mid-session). Manual check next session: spawn a search subagent, then confirm ~/.claude/telemetry/coverage/<that-agent-id>.json appears.

title: Phase0 T2: Define provenance handoff schema (spec)
description: Author reference doc ~/.claude/references/handoff-provenance.md defining the producer/consumer contract. Context files: ~/.claude/agents/search.md:66-78 (existing 4-section report format), ~/.claude/agents/orchestrator.md:151-156 (UNVERIFIED convention).
acceptance_criteria: Reference doc defines: (1) 3-level confidence taxonomy extending UNVERIFIED ([verified]=direct cited evidence, [inferred]=reasoned, [unverified]=uncheckable); (2) mandatory PREMISE CHECK field (Y/N + detail); (3) COVERAGE block, explicitly secondary to telemetry-observed coverage; (4) consumer rules. Self-contained and citable from the three agent files.

title: Phase0 T1: Determine non-destructive PreToolUse gate signal
description: Explore whether Claude Code PreToolUse hooks support a non-deny advisory output (permissionDecision=ask, additionalContext, or stdout surfaced to the model) vs deny-only. Recommend an acknowledgement pattern for the coverage gate (T5). Context: ~/.claude/hooks/enforce-orchestrator.sh (marker-file ack precedent); ~/.claude/tools/claude-hook-guard/src/input.rs:60-84 (deny-only output struct).
acceptance_criteria: Written determination with cited source covering: (1) do PreToolUse hooks support non-blocking advisory output reaching the model, or only allow/deny/ask; (2) UX of ask; (3) cleanest ack mechanism (marker file vs env vs task-embedded) so a worker editing outside observed coverage is warned/asked once, not hard-blocked. If only deny/ask exist, specify which to use.

title: Phase0 review polish: coverage pattern privacy + minor cleanups
description: Should-fix findings from the T8 final review (no blockers): (1) PRIVACY: hooked coverage persists Glob/Grep pattern strings verbatim to ~/.claude/telemetry/coverage/<id>.json (coverage.rs ~lines 75-83); patterns can contain sensitive search literals. Decide: document accepted risk (local-only dir) vs redact/truncate patterns vs make pattern-persistence opt-in. (2) coverage-autowrite.sh: HOOKED_BIN is a hardcoded absolute path that silently no-ops if moved; consider deriving from $HOME or documenting intent. (3) provenance.rs lines 134-136: truncation-marker guard is redundant (file_path absent in {_t,_b} markers already causes skip); add a clarifying comment or remove.
acceptance_criteria: Privacy decision made and implemented or explicitly documented in handoff-provenance.md; the two minor cleanups addressed or consciously declined with a note.
notes: Add from T8 security review of claude-hook-guard/src/command_match.rs (verdict: NO bypass; safe): (4) add a guard for a dangling -C/--directory as the final token (currently yields bare ["bd"]); (5) add unit tests for edge cases ["bd","-C"] (dangling) and ["git","-C","/a","-C","/b","status"] (repeated). Non-exploitable robustness only.
